relations.note: Letter-tint device refines the base campaign headline treatment.
Lockup copy accompanies the campaign headline treatment.

quotes: Two approved colorways: brand **Gold/Sunshine** or **Brand Blue**
Stack as three lines (YOU / DESERVE / MORE) in hero placements, left-aligned.
Set in **Agenda** (digital may use Agenda as live text for this headline only; email fallback: Nunito Sans ExtraBold).
Two approved colorways: brand **Gold/Sunshine** or **Brand Blue** (tinted letters at 40% of the same hue).
Stack as three lines (YOU / DESERVE / MORE) in hero placements, left-aligned.

rules.intent: Preserve the locked campaign headline treatment.
rules.rule_text: The "YOU DESERVE MORE" campaign headline is set in Agenda (campaign_headline.font = Agenda); digital may use Agenda as live text for this headline only, with email fallback Nunito Sans ExtraBold. It is ALL CAPS, always (case.campaign_headline.all_caps). Two approved colorways only: brand Gold/Sunshine (#ffc60a/#faa31b) or Brand Blue (#00529b). In hero placements it stacks as three lines (YOU / DESERVE / MORE), left-aligned (alignment.campaign_headline.left = left).

quotes: set at approximately 40% tint** of the headline color
The letters **"D" (in DESERVE) and "M" (in MORE) are set at approximately 40% tint** of the headline color — a nod to the "d" and "m" of dermatomyositis.
(tinted letters at 40% of the same hue)

rules.intent: Encode the intentional dermatomyositis letter-tint device.
rules.rule_text: In the "YOU DESERVE MORE" campaign headline, the letters "D" (in DESERVE) and "M" (in MORE) are set at approximately 40% tint of the headline color (opacity.headline.letter_tint_40 = 0.4) — a nod to the "d" and "m" of dermatomyositis. Tinted letters use 40% of the same hue as the chosen colorway.

effect.content: YOU DESERVE MORE. So there's LISRAYA—the first targeted treatment for dermatomyositis.

quotes: **YOU DESERVE MORE.** So there's LISRAYA—the first targeted treatment for dermatomyositis.
Full lockup copy: "**YOU DESERVE MORE.** So there's LISRAYA—the first targeted treatment for dermatomyositis."
